metadata.purpose: Mathematical shortcuts for attention mechanism optimization in edge LLM inference

core_mathematics.linear_attention: phi(Q) * (phi(K)^T * V) where phi is a kernel function

kernel_functions.name: Random Fourier Features (RFF)
kernel_functions.approximation_quality: Good for long sequences, degrades on short sequences

kernel_functions.name: Positive Random Features
kernel_functions.approximation_quality: Better numerical stability, used in Performer

alternative_approaches.name: Linear Transformer
alternative_approaches.mechanism: Feature map phi(x) = elu(x) + 1, decomposes softmax

alternative_approaches.name: Fast Attention Via Orthogonal Random Features (FAVOR+)
alternative_approaches.mechanism: Random feature approximation of softmax kernel

alternative_approaches.name: RWKV
alternative_approaches.mechanism: RNN-like linear attention, no KV cache needed

quality_tradeoff.long_range_dependency: Degraded - linear attention loses precision on long-range tokens
quality_tradeoff.training_instability: Requires careful initialization, learning rate scheduling
quality_tradeoff.in_context_learning: Reduced capability compared to softmax attention

hardware_implication.memory_reduction: O(n) KV cache vs O(n^2) attention matrix
hardware_implication.edge_benefit: Enables 10K+ context length with fixed memory

for_bitnet.applicable: Yes - can combine with ternary weights
for_bitnet.challenge: Kernel functions need higher precision than ternary

core_concept.problem: Standard attention materializes O(n^2) attention matrix in HBM
core_concept.solution: Tiled computation keeping attention matrix in SRAM

inference_specific.benefit: Reduced memory bandwidth for prompt processing

hardware_requirements.on_chip_sram: Need ~1-2 MB per attention head for tiling

for_edge_fpga.implementation: Use BRAM tiles for attention computation

for_mask_locked_chip.rationale: Weight access already eliminated, but activation bandwidth still matters

implementation_notes.rescaling: Online softmax requires careful numerical handling
implementation_notes.backward_pass: Flash Attention includes memory-efficient backward (for training)

sparse_patterns.description: Each token attends only to W previous tokens
sparse_patterns.memory_reduction: W/n where W is window size, n is sequence length

quality_impact.reason: Most attention is local in practice

implementations: Longformer uses sliding window + global tokens

sparse_patterns.description: Attend to every k-th token, different dilation per head

quality_impact.reason: Can miss important tokens if dilation poorly chosen

sparse_patterns.description: Mix of local attention (window) and global tokens (CLS, special)
sparse_patterns.memory_reduction: Depends on ratio of global to local

sparse_patterns.description: Attention in blocks, with sparse block selection

quality_impact.reason: Learned patterns work well but fixed patterns may miss

sparse_patterns.description: Keep initial tokens (attention sinks) + sliding window
sparse_patterns.memory_reduction: (W + S)/n where S is sink tokens, W is window

quality_impact.reason: Attention naturally focuses on initial tokens (sink phenomenon)
quality_impact.key_insight: First few tokens 'absorb' attention scores, preventing collapse

sparse_patterns.enables: Infinite context length with fixed memory

sparse_patterns.description: Use locality-sensitive hashing to group similar queries
sparse_patterns.memory_reduction: O(n log n) instead of O(n^2)

description.mqa: Multi-Query Attention - all heads share single K, V
description.gqa: Grouped-Query Attention - groups of heads share K, V

bitnet_status.details: BitNet b1.58 2B4T uses 32 query heads with 4 KV heads (GQA-8)

kv_cache_reduction.mqa: Reduce KV cache by h where h = number of attention heads
kv_cache_reduction.gqa: Reduce KV cache by h/g where g = number of KV head groups

quality_impact.gqa: Minimal degradation, close to full attention quality
quality_impact.studies: GQA with 8 groups matches MHA quality in most benchmarks

for_existing_models.uptraining: Can convert MHA to GQA with small fine-tuning
for_existing_models.procedure: 1. Cluster similar attention heads, 2. Average their weights, 3. Fine-tune

hardware_implication.memory_bandwidth: Reduced proportionally to KV head reduction
hardware_implication.compute: Unchanged - still compute all Q heads
hardware_implication.edge_benefit: Critical for long context - enables 4-8x longer sequences

core_mechanism.discovery: Attention scores concentrate on initial tokens (softmax normalization)
core_mechanism.problem: Evicting initial tokens causes attention collapse
core_mechanism.solution: Keep initial 'sink' tokens in KV cache permanently

mathematical_explanation.softmax_property: Softmax outputs sum to 1, needs 'anchor' tokens
mathematical_explanation.sink_tokens: Initial tokens become attention sinks, absorbing scores
mathematical_explanation.without_sinks: Evicting sinks causes attention weights to become uniform (collapse)

streaming_llm.method: Rolling KV cache: keep sink tokens + recent window

limitations: Quality depends on sink token count

memory_savings.example: 4 sinks + 4096 window = constant 4100 tokens in KV cache

hardware_implementation.fifo_buffer: Simple circular buffer for recent window

for_bitnet.benefit: Combined with ternary weights, enables very efficient inference

approaches.name: INT8 QKV Quantization
approaches.description: Quantize Query, Key, Value matrices to INT8
approaches.hardware_benefit: 2x memory reduction, 2-4x compute speedup with INT8 units

approaches.name: KV Cache Quantization
approaches.description: Quantize stored K, V cache to lower precision
approaches.for_edge: Critical - KV cache dominates memory for long sequences

approaches.name: Logarithmic Attention
approaches.benefit: Avoids numerical overflow, enables lower precision
approaches.challenge: log and exp operations expensive in hardware

approaches.name: Ternary Attention Weights

feasibility.reason: Softmax produces continuous distribution, hard to ternarize

alternatives: Top-k sparsification (keep top k attention scores)

approaches.name: Sparsemax Attention
approaches.mechanism: sparsemax(z) = argmin_p ||p - z||^2 s.t. p in probability simplex
approaches.quality_impact: Similar to softmax for most tasks

attention_computation.qk_multiplication: Ternary @ INT8 -> can use popcount-based computation

bitnet_specific.unique_benefit: Ternary weights eliminate weight memory access entirely

multi_query_attention_variants.name: Multi-Query Attention (MQA)
multi_query_attention_variants.description: Single KV head shared across all query heads

multi_query_attention_variants.name: Grouped-Query Attention (GQA)
multi_query_attention_variants.description: Groups of query heads share one KV head
multi_query_attention_variants.quality_tradeoff: Minimal, bridges MQA and MHA quality

multi_query_attention_variants.name: Claude's Multi-Query
multi_query_attention_variants.notes: Claude models use MQA for efficiency

paged_attention.description: KV cache stored in non-contiguous memory pages

benefit.sharing: Share KV cache across sequences (beam search)
benefit.paging: Can offload to CPU memory when not needed

paged_attention.hardware_implication: Requires memory management unit, not trivial in ASIC

speculative_decoding_attention.description: Verify multiple draft tokens in single attention pass
speculative_decoding_attention.benefit: Amortize attention overhead across multiple tokens

recommended_approach.justification: 4x KV cache reduction with minimal quality loss. BitNet already uses this. Standard in modern LLMs (Llama 3, etc.). Hardware complexity is low.
recommended_approach.implementation: Native training with GQA, or uptrain existing MHA models

recommended_approach.justification: 2x additional memory reduction. Minimal quality impact. Simple implementation. Works with GQA for compound savings.
recommended_approach.implementation: Per-token absmax quantization, calibrate on validation set

recommended_approach.justification: 8-10x memory bandwidth reduction during prompt processing. Critical for long prompts. Well-understood implementation.
recommended_approach.implementation: Tile attention computation in on-chip SRAM/BRAM

recommended_approach.justification: Enables fixed memory for long conversations. Simple to implement. Compatible with all other techniques.
recommended_approach.implementation: Reserve 4 initial token slots, use circular buffer for window

recommended_approach.justification: Additional memory reduction when full context not needed. Combine with attention sink for best results.
recommended_approach.implementation: Window size 2048-4096 based on task requirements

combination_analysis.hardware_complexity: Medium - all techniques have proven implementations

additional_optimizations: Add INT8 KV cache quantization (BitNet.cpp implements this)
Implement Flash Attention for prefill phase
Add attention sink for streaming applications

mask_locked_specific.remaining_bottleneck: KV cache and activation memory bandwidth
mask_locked_specific.priority: KV cache optimization + Flash Attention for activations

tasks: Implement Flash Attention tiling in hardware

linear_attention: Performer: Rethinking Attention with Performers (Choromanski et al., 2020)
Linear Transformers Are Secretly Fast Weight Programmers (Katharopoulos et al., 2020)
RWKV: Reinventing RNNs for the Transformer Era (Peng et al., 2023)
flash_attention: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention (Dao et al., 2022)
FlashAttention-2: Faster Attention with Better Parallelism and Work Partitioning (Dao, 2023)
sparse_attention: Longformer: The Long-Document Transformer (Beltagy et al., 2020)
BigBird: Transformers for Longer Sequences (Zaheer et al., 2020)
StreamingLLM: Efficient Streaming Language Models with Attention Sinks (Xiao et al., 2023)
mqa_gqa: Fast Transformer Decoding: One Write-Head is All You Need (Shazeer, 2019)
GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models (Ainslie et al., 2023)
attention_sink: StreamingLLM: Efficient Streaming Language Models (Xiao et al., 2023)
quantized_attention: BitNet b1.58: The Era of 1-bit LLMs (Ma et al., 2024)
